Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Entered Against Defendant City And County Of San Francisco
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff WILLIAM BAILEY (Tel: 415-240-9191) in pro per;
Atty. AMY P. FRENZEN, Esq. of San Francisco City Attorney's Office (Tel: 415-554-3885) for defendant San Francisco Sheriff's Department.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Set Aside Default Entered Against Defendant City And County Of San Francisco.

Matter discussed and submitted.

As to the plaintiff's request to set a settlement conference, defense counsel indicate they are agreeable to participating in a settlement conference. The Court indicates it will make an inquiry into having a settlement conference set in this case.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's ("the City") motion to vacate the default entered on December 29, 2020 is granted.

A motion to vacate a default based on CCP 473(d) [void default] can be brought at any time. (See Manson, Iver & York v. Black (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 36, 43 [" A judgment void on its face may be set aside on motion without any time limitation."]; County of San Diego v. Gorham (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 1215, 1225-1226 [judgment can be vacated "at any time" where court lacks jurisdiction].)

Here, the default is void on its face and vacated. The proof of service entered on December 10, 2020 shows that plaintiff served the San Francisco Sheriff's Department. But that department is a constituent of the City and plaintiff was required to serve the Mayor's office. (See SF Charter, Art. 1, sec.1.101; Art. III, sec. 3.100.) The defective service renders the default void.

The City shall file its proposed answer by July 29, 2022. The OSC set for August 2, 2022, in department 610 is off calendar as moot.

Judge: Vedica Puri; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/VP)
